restaurants.name: Parea Tavern
restaurants.ambience: Cozy, family-friendly tavern with a warm, Mediterranean feel—relaxed indoor and outdoor seating at the heart of Fira
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

restaurants.name: Ouzeri
restaurants.ambience: Casual Greek ouzeri just off the main walkway—no caldera view, but a genuine local experience with outdoor seating and friendly service
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

attractions.name: Fira Town
attractions.description: Lively and vibrant, with narrow lanes, white buildings, and endless viewpoints overlooking the caldera

attractions.name: Oia Village
attractions.description: This is the Santorini you've seen in postcards — blue domes, whitewashed homes, and dramatic cliffside views

attractions.name: Santorini Cable Car
attractions.description: This short but scenic ride lifts you up dramatic cliffs into Fira, offering breathtaking views over the caldera as you ascend

attractions.name: Fira
attractions.description: Capital of Santorini, full of shops and cafés, Cable car down to the old port or walk the caldera path
attractions.entry_fee: 6 euros for one-way cable car ticket
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

attractions.name: Oia
attractions.description: Gorgeous cliffside village, blue domes and postcard-perfect scenery
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

phrases: Dress modestly when visiting churches and monasteries - cover shoulders and knees
Greeks typically eat dinner very late (9-11 PM), so restaurants may seem empty early in the evening
Tipping 10-15% is appreciated at restaurants, and rounding up for taxi drivers is common
Avoid pointing with your index finger - use an open hand instead, as pointing is considered rude
safety_tips: INDIAN EMBASSY DETAILS: Indian Embassy in Athens covers Santorini. Address: 2-4 Kleanthous Street, 10675 Athens. Phone: +30-210-7257200. Emergency: +30-6944-101010. Email: cons.athens@mea.gov.in
EMERGENCY NUMBERS: Police: 100, Ambulance/Medical Emergency: 166, Fire Department: 199, Tourist Police: 171, European Emergency Number: 112
LOCAL POLICE: Santorini Police Station - Fira: +30-22860-22649. Always carry passport/ID and keep copies separate from originals
MEDICAL FACILITIES: Santorini General Hospital, Fira: +30-22860-23000. Private clinics available in Fira and Oia. Ensure travel insurance covers medical evacuation
COMMON SCAMS: Overcharging at restaurants (always check prices), fake jewelry sellers, taxi meter manipulation, accommodation booking scams online, ATM skimming devices
TRANSPORTATION SAFETY: Use licensed taxis with meters, avoid unlicensed ATV rentals, be cautious on narrow cliff roads, wear helmets on scooters/ATVs
WATER SAFETY: Strong currents at beaches, no lifeguards at many locations, avoid swimming alone, check weather conditions before boat trips
CLIFF SAFETY: Stay on marked paths near caldera cliffs, avoid cliff edges during windy weather, be extra careful during sunset viewing in crowded areas
MONEY SAFETY: Use ATMs inside banks when possible, notify your bank of travel plans, carry backup payment methods, avoid displaying large amounts of cash
ACCOMMODATION SAFETY: Book through reputable platforms, verify property exists before arrival, keep valuables in hotel safe, check emergency exits
FOOD SAFETY: Drink bottled water, eat at busy restaurants with high turnover, be cautious with seafood in extreme heat, check food hygiene ratings
CULTURAL CONSIDERATIONS: Dress modestly when visiting churches/monasteries, respect photography restrictions, be mindful of noise levels in residential areas
NATURAL HAZARDS: Volcanic island with occasional seismic activity, strong winds especially in summer, flash floods possible during rare heavy rains
COMMUNICATION: Download offline maps, learn basic Greek phrases, keep embassy contact info saved, inform family of your itinerary and check-in regularly
connectivity_tips: Purchase a Cosmote prepaid SIM card at Athens airport or Santorini airport for €10-15 with 5-10GB data. Cosmote has the best coverage across Greek islands including Santorini.
Wind Hellas offers competitive prepaid plans starting at €8 for 4GB data. Available at Wind stores in Fira and Oia, or purchase online before arrival.
Vodafone Greece provides reliable coverage in Santorini with prepaid plans from €10 for 6GB. Buy SIM cards at Vodafone stores in Fira or at the airport.
Consider eSIM options like Airalo or Holafly for convenience - no physical SIM needed. Plans start at $4.50 for 1GB valid for 7 days, perfect for short trips.
Orange Holiday Europe eSIM covers Greece and 30+ European countries for €24.99 with 8GB data, ideal if visiting multiple European destinations.
Coverage quality is excellent in main towns like Fira, Oia, and Kamari. Expect slower speeds in remote areas like Akrotiri archaeological site or secluded beaches.
Buy SIM cards at Athens airport duty-free shops, Santorini airport, or mobile operator stores in Fira. Avoid hotel purchases as they charge premium rates.
Bring your passport for SIM card registration - it's mandatory in Greece. The activation process takes 5-10 minutes at official stores.
Free WiFi is widely available at hotels, restaurants, and cafes in tourist areas. Use WiFi for heavy data usage and mobile data for navigation and emergencies.
Download offline maps on Google Maps before arriving to save data. Santorini's compact size means you can navigate most areas without constant internet access.
Consider roaming add-on packs from Indian carriers like Airtel (€3-5 per day) or Jio (€4-6 per day) for short stays under 5 days to avoid SIM card hassles.
Top-up credit at convenience stores, supermarkets, or online. Most plans offer unlimited local calls and SMS within Greece along with data allowances.
health_tips: No specific vaccinations are required for Indian travelers entering Greece/Santorini, as Greece does not mandate any vaccines for visitors from India
Ensure routine vaccinations are up-to-date: MMR (measles-mumps-rubella), DPT (diphtheria-pertussis-tetanus), chickenpox, polio, and yearly flu shot
Recommended vaccines: Hepatitis A and B (especially Hep A for food/water protection), and Typhoid if you plan to eat at local tavernas frequently
Consider Tick-borne Encephalitis vaccine only if planning extensive hiking or camping in rural areas during spring/summer months
Tap water in Santorini is generally safe but heavily chlorinated and may taste unpleasant - bottled water is widely available and recommended
Be cautious with seafood during hot summer months - choose busy restaurants with high turnover and avoid raw or undercooked fish
Pack sunscreen (SPF 30+), hat, and sunglasses - the Aegean sun is intense, especially when reflected off white buildings and sea
Bring basic medications from India as familiar brands may not be available - include anti-diarrheal, pain relievers, and any prescription medicines
Summer temperatures can reach 35°C+ - stay hydrated, seek shade during midday (11am-4pm), and wear light, breathable clothing
Santorini has excellent medical facilities and pharmacies - European Health Insurance Card not applicable to Indian citizens, so ensure comprehensive travel insurance
Swimming safety: beaches have strong currents and winds - swim at organized beaches with lifeguards when possible
No malaria risk in Greece - mosquito-borne diseases are minimal, but pack insect repellent for evening outdoor dining
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf
